title: David laments Saul and Jonathan
summary_text: A messenger brings David news of Saul and Jonathan's death, hoping for reward, but instead he meets grief and judgment. David composes a lament that honors Saul as Israel's fallen king and Jonathan as his beloved friend. The chapter begins David's reign with sorrow rather than triumph.
key_points: The messenger claims to have finished Saul off.
David rejects the reward-seeking report.
David mourns Saul and Jonathan in song.
The Amalekite is executed for boasting.
The chapter frames kingship with lament.
evidence_phrases: How are the mighty fallen
I am distressed for you, my brother Jonathan
I have slain the LORD's anointed
the beauty of Israel is slain
the song of the bow

title: David in Hebron and civil division
summary_text: David seeks the LORD and goes to Hebron, where Judah anoints him king, while Abner makes Ish-bosheth king over the rest of Israel. The chapter's conflicts begin with a brutal contest at Gibeon and end with Asahel's death and Joab's hard-won victory. It shows a kingdom split before it is unified.
key_points: David begins reigning from Hebron.
Abner installs Ish-bosheth over Israel.
A deadly contest erupts at Gibeon.
Asahel is killed in the chase.
The war between houses deepens.
evidence_phrases: go up to Hebron
David was made king over the house of Judah
let the young men arise
turn aside to your right hand or to your left
the war was very severe

title: Abner defects and dies
summary_text: Abner turns against Ish-bosheth and promises to bring all Israel to David, but Joab kills him in revenge for Asahel's death. David distances himself from the murder, laments Abner publicly, and refuses to let his own house appear guilty. The chapter strengthens David's legitimacy while exposing the violence around him.
key_points: Abner defects from Ish-bosheth to David.
Joab murders Abner in secret.
David refuses responsibility for the killing.
He mourns Abner before the people.
The chapter advances David's legitimacy.
evidence_phrases: make your covenant with me
I am this day weak
he died for his brother Asahel's blood
the king lifted up his voice
died as a fool dies

title: Ish-bosheth murdered
summary_text: When Ish-bosheth's captains bring him the news of Abner's death, two men murder him in his house and flee with his head. David again refuses to reward violence, executing the assassins and honoring the slain king with burial. The chapter removes Saul's last rival by treachery rather than battle.
key_points: Ish-bosheth is murdered in his own house.
The killers expect reward from David.
David executes them for their crime.
Ish-bosheth's head is buried with honor.
Saul's dynasty continues to unravel.
evidence_phrases: they took his head
slew the wicked person in his own house
I will require his blood
they buried the head of Ish-bosheth
the kingdom was confirmed in the hand of David

title: David captures Jerusalem
summary_text: The tribes finally come together and anoint David king over all Israel at Hebron. David captures Jerusalem from the Jebusites, makes it his stronghold, and grows stronger as the LORD establishes him. Philistine attacks follow, but David seeks God's guidance and wins decisive victories.
key_points: All Israel accepts David as king.
Jerusalem becomes David's city.
The LORD establishes David's kingdom.
David defeats two Philistine assaults.
He seeks God before each campaign.
evidence_phrases: bone and flesh
David took the stronghold of Zion
the LORD established him king
David inquired of the LORD
the LORD has broken out on my enemies

title: The ark enters Jerusalem
summary_text: David brings the ark toward Jerusalem, but Uzzah dies when he touches it, forcing David to slow down and rethink reverence. The ark rests in Obed-edom's house, then returns with joy as David dances before the LORD while Michal despises him. The chapter balances celebration with the fear of holy things.
key_points: Uzzah dies when he touches the ark.
The ark blesses Obed-edom's house.
David dances before the LORD in Jerusalem.
Michal despises David for his worship.
The chapter demands both joy and reverence.
evidence_phrases: the LORD had broken forth upon Uzzah
David danced before the LORD with all his might
the ark of the LORD remained
Michal despised him in her heart
I will be yet more vile than this

title: The Davidic covenant
summary_text: David wants to build a house for the LORD, but the LORD instead promises to build David a lasting house and kingdom. Nathan relays the covenant that will outlast David's reign and tie his line to future kingship. David responds with humility and awe, recognizing that God has spoken grace beyond his request.
key_points: David wants to build a temple.
God reverses the plan and promises David a house.
The covenant points to an enduring throne.
Nathan delivers the divine word.
David prays in gratitude and humility.
evidence_phrases: the LORD will make you a house
your throne shall be established forever
Who am I, O Lord GOD
according to your own heart
the word that you have spoken

title: David's victories and officers
summary_text: David defeats the Philistines, Moab, Hadadezer, the Arameans, and the Edomites, securing tribute from many surrounding peoples. He dedicates the spoils to the LORD and appoints officers over his expanding kingdom. The chapter shows a monarch whose victories are disciplined by worship and administration.
key_points: David wins battles on several fronts.
He dedicates spoils to the LORD.
Neighboring nations become subject to him.
Officials are appointed for stable rule.
The kingdom grows through disciplined victory.
evidence_phrases: the LORD gave victory to David wherever he went
David dedicated them to the LORD
he put garrisons in Edom
David reigned over all Israel
Joab was over the army

title: Mephibosheth at David's table
summary_text: David searches for anyone from Saul's house to show covenant kindness for Jonathan's sake and finds Mephibosheth, Jonathan's crippled son. He restores Saul's land to him and gives him a permanent place at the king's table. The chapter turns royal power into mercy and memory.
key_points: David seeks a survivor of Saul's house.
Mephibosheth is brought from hiding.
Saul's land is returned to him.
He eats regularly at David's table.
Kindness is extended for Jonathan's sake.
evidence_phrases: Is there yet any left
for Jonathan's sake
the lame son of Jonathan
he shall eat bread at my table
like one of the king's sons

title: Ammonite and Aramean war
summary_text: After insult to David's envoys, the Ammonites hire Aramean allies and prepare for war. Joab divides the army, the battle turns when the enemy retreats, and David's forces follow up with a larger campaign against the Arameans. The chapter shows David's kingdom defending its honor and gaining strength through coordinated leadership.
key_points: The Ammonites humiliate David's servants.
Joab divides his forces for the battle.
The enemy coalition falls back in fear.
The Arameans regroup and are defeated again.
David's kingdom proves resilient.
evidence_phrases: they hired the Arameans
Be strong, and let us show ourselves courageous
the Arameans fled
the LORD preserved David wherever he went
the kings made peace with Israel

title: Bathsheba and Uriah
summary_text: While Joab is at war, David stays behind, sees Bathsheba, and commits adultery that leads into deception and murder. Uriah refuses the comforts of home while his comrades fight, and David arranges his death in battle to cover the sin. The chapter is the moral turning point of David's reign.
key_points: David sends for Bathsheba.
He tries to hide the pregnancy through Uriah.
Uriah refuses personal comfort during war.
David orders Uriah placed in danger.
The chapter ends with sin concealed from others but not from God.
evidence_phrases: David sent messengers and took her
the matter that David had done displeased the LORD
my lord Joab and the servants of my lord are encamped
set him in the forefront of the hottest battle
the thing that David had done

title: Nathan rebukes David
summary_text: Nathan confronts David with a parable, and David condemns the rich man's theft before realizing he is the guilty one. God announces judgment on David's house, yet the king repents and receives mercy as Bathsheba bears Solomon. The chapter joins severe discipline with forgiveness and restored worship.
key_points: Nathan's parable exposes David's crime.
David confesses his sin.
The child born from the adultery dies.
David worships after the child dies.
Solomon is born after repentance.
evidence_phrases: you are the man
I have sinned against the LORD
the LORD has also put away your sin
the child died
he called his name Jedidiah

title: Amnon and Tamar
summary_text: Amnon lusts after his half-sister Tamar, deceives her, and then hates her after violating her. Absalom shelters Tamar and quietly begins to nurse hatred against Amnon, while David is angry but passive. The chapter opens the final collapse of David's family life.
key_points: Amnon schemes to isolate Tamar.
He violates her and then despises her.
Absalom shelters Tamar in grief.
David is angry but does not act.
The family fracture deepens.
evidence_phrases: I love Tamar, my brother Absalom's sister
he forced her
he hated her with very great hatred
hold now your peace
Absalom hated Amnon

title: Absalom returns to Jerusalem
summary_text: Joab uses a wise woman from Tekoa to press David to think about bringing back Absalom. David relents, but when Absalom is restored he remains half-healed and unsatisfied, living in Jerusalem yet not seeing the king's face. The chapter shows reconciliation managed politically rather than spiritually.
key_points: Joab engineers a parable to move David.
Absalom is brought back to Jerusalem.
He remains cut off from the king's face.
His beauty and pride are emphasized.
The chapter prepares for a larger revolt.
evidence_phrases: the woman of Tekoa
bring the young man Absalom back
let him turn to his own house
he saw not the king's face
there was no blemish in him

title: Absalom's conspiracy and David's flight
summary_text: Absalom wins hearts in Israel and stages a conspiracy that forces David to flee Jerusalem barefoot and grieving. Loyal servants, priests, and counselors all face hard choices as the king leaves the city. The chapter shows the cost of sin rippling outward into public collapse.
key_points: Absalom steals the people's hearts.
David flees Jerusalem with his household.
Priests and friends must decide where to stand.
Hushai stays behind to frustrate counsel.
The rebellion becomes a national crisis.
evidence_phrases: Absalom stole the hearts of the men of Israel
arise and let us flee
the king went forth barefoot
I will be your servant
the counsel of Ahithophel

title: Ziba, Shimei, and Hushai
summary_text: As David escapes, Ziba opportunistically claims Mephibosheth's loyalty, and Shimei curses David with stones and dust. David refuses revenge and leaves judgment to God while Hushai arrives in Jerusalem to counter Ahithophel's counsel. The chapter is full of mixed motives, but David keeps moving with restraint.
key_points: Ziba brings provisions and a sharp accusation.
Shimei curses David along the road.
David refuses to retaliate against Shimei.
Hushai joins Absalom as a spy.
The chapter highlights God's hidden governance.
evidence_phrases: Shimei cursed as he went
let him curse
the LORD has said to him, Curse David
I will serve you
the counsel of Ahithophel

title: Ahithophel and Hushai
summary_text: Ahithophel gives Absalom a ruthless plan to strike David immediately, but Hushai gives a slower counsel designed to buy David time. The warning is carried through loyal messengers, and when Ahithophel sees his counsel rejected he goes home and hangs himself. The chapter shows wisdom fighting wisdom while God protects David through delay.
key_points: Ahithophel proposes an immediate strike.
Hushai counters with a broader plan.
Messengers warn David to escape again.
Ahithophel's counsel is rejected.
David gains precious time.
evidence_phrases: the counsel of Ahithophel
the LORD had ordained to defeat the good counsel
go quickly and tell David
put your hand in your own throat
the evil is determined upon David

title: Absalom dies in the forest
summary_text: David's forces defeat Absalom's army in the forest of Ephraim, and Absalom is caught in an oak by his hair. Joab ignores David's plea and kills Absalom, ending the rebellion with a public heap of stones and a private father's grief. The chapter is a grim climax where victory and sorrow collide.
key_points: David's army wins the forest battle.
Absalom is trapped by his hair.
Joab kills him despite orders.
A memorial heap marks his death.
David mourns the son's rebellion deeply.
evidence_phrases: the battle was in the forest
his head caught fast in the oak
I may not tarry thus with you
my son Absalom
a great heap of stones

title: David returns to Jerusalem
summary_text: Joab rebukes David for grieving so hard that he seems to dishonor the men who saved him, and the king returns to Jerusalem after political confusion in Judah and Israel. The chapter includes tensions with Mephibosheth, the faithful support of Barzillai, and disputes over who truly stood by the king. David comes back to power, but the kingdom is still strained.
key_points: Joab forces David to face the people.
Judah and Israel argue over who gets priority.
Mephibosheth defends his loyalty.
Barzillai is honored for his kindness.
Shimei is spared and the return is incomplete.
evidence_phrases: you have today shamed the faces of all your servants
bring back the king
I am a dead dog
let me now die in my own city
why have you become the last to bring back the king

title: Sheba's revolt and Amasa's death
summary_text: Sheba son of Bichri sparks a northern revolt, and David's new general Amasa is murdered by Joab under a pretense of greeting. The rebellion is contained after Joab pursues Sheba to Abel Beth-maacah, where a wise woman negotiates peace by handing over Sheba's head. The chapter shows how fragile David's restored kingdom still is.
key_points: Sheba ignites another rebellion.
Joab murders Amasa on the road.
David's forces pursue the rebel north.
A wise woman saves a city by negotiation.
The revolt ends with Sheba's death.
evidence_phrases: we have no portion in David
the sword that is in your hand
the wise woman cried
they cut off the head of Sheba
Joab returned to Jerusalem

title: Famine, Rizpah, and the giants
summary_text: A famine reveals bloodguilt against the Gibeonites, so David hands over descendants of Saul and the land receives relief after justice is done. Rizpah's vigil over the bodies moves David to gather Saul and Jonathan's bones, and the chapter closes with battles against Philistine giants. Mercy, memory, and continued warfare all stand together here.
key_points: A famine exposes Saul's bloodguilt.
Rizpah keeps watch over the dead.
David gathers the bones of Saul and Jonathan.
Israel wins further battles against Philistine giants.
Justice and remembrance end the famine.
evidence_phrases: it is because of Saul
Rizpah the daughter of Aiah
gathered the bones of Saul
the LORD heeded the prayer for the land
these four were born to the giant

title: David's song of deliverance
summary_text: David sings a long psalm of rescue, praising the LORD as rock, fortress, and deliverer who answered him in distress. The song celebrates God's intervention in battle, his perfect ways, and his care for the humble. It becomes David's theological summary of why he survived and ruled.
key_points: David praises God for rescue.
The LORD is described in many strong images.
God equips David for battle and victory.
The song recalls deliverance from enemies.
The psalm frames David's reign as grace.
evidence_phrases: the LORD is my rock
my shield
he sent from on high
he brought me forth also into a large place
the LORD lives

title: David's last words and mighty men
summary_text: David's last words celebrate the righteous ruler who governs in the fear of God and shines like the morning light. The chapter then lists his mighty men, honoring the loyal warriors who shared his hard-won victories. It closes David's life story with both ideal kingship and remembered companionship.
key_points: David reflects on righteous rule.
He describes covenant leadership as life-giving.
The mighty men are remembered by name and deed.
Their courage is tied to loyalty.
The chapter honors the king's support network.
evidence_phrases: the Spirit of the LORD spoke by me
he that rules over men must be just
the three mighty men
eleven in the harvest field
the mighty men whom David had

title: The census and the plague
summary_text: David orders a census, then recognizes that the counting was sinful and chooses to fall into the LORD's hand rather than man's. A plague strikes Israel until David builds an altar on Araunah's threshing floor and offers sacrifices. The chapter ends with judgment checked by mercy and a site that will later matter deeply in Israel's worship life.
key_points: David's census brings guilt.
He chooses the LORD's mercy over human hands.
A plague falls on Israel.
David buys Araunah's threshing floor.
The altar stops the judgment.
evidence_phrases: I have sinned greatly in that I have done
let us fall now into the hand of the LORD
the LORD was entreated for the land
build an altar to the LORD
the plague was stayed from Israel
